description: 'wireo' gathers time after the present and events that have not happened. As a noun, it lets the future itself become a subject of thought or speech; the particle 'so' separately places a clause there.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then release into high-front unrounded 'i'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue at the alveolar ridge and continue into stressed middle-height front 'e'. Keep the final rounded middle-height 'o' separate across the vowel hiatus.

semantic_domains.temporal: later time made available as a subject of thought or action
